commit_message: perf(pipeline): defer probe-side scans with unmet FULL-barrier
label: preloaded-scan-region SF=100 saturation map
notes: 66 single-warm-iter captures (22 queries x 3 GPU configs). At SF=100 dec_region preload OOMs on 8 queries (q1,5,6,8,9,18,21 + q19); for q1/5/6/8/9/18/21 both preload AND query bad_alloc, so the .nsys-rep is metadata-only (~430KB, zero CUPTI events because the allocator fails before any kernel launches — e.g. cudf::concatenate requesting 36GB on a 40GB A100). For q19 the preload OOMs but the cold/warm iters then run on the NATIVE scan path (silent fallback), so dec_region q19's kernel/h2d numbers in nsys_summary.csv are essentially native q19's numbers — exclude it from any 'real dec_region' aggregate. enc_region OOMs only on q9. Real dec_region intersection (queries where dec actually used preloaded data): 14 queries [2,3,4,7,10,11,12,13,14,15,16,17,20,22]. Aggregate over those 14: native kernel=4006ms, enc=4212ms (0.95x), dec=3166ms (1.27x); h2d native=64.7GB, enc=361MB, dec=19MB. KEY FINDING: enc_region kernel time is essentially identical to native because enc still runs the same DECODE kernels (BITPACKING/RLE/ALP unpack) — preload only removes the H2D *transfer* of encoded blocks, not the decode work itself. dec_region wins kernel time (1.27x) because cudf::concatenate replaces decode entirely. enc's value is wall-time/H2D-bandwidth savings (180x reduction in H2D), NOT GPU kernel savings. The original 22Q aggregate at SF=20 (enc=1.45x, dec=1.49x) was driven by lineitem-dominated heavy queries (Q1,5,6,8,9,18,21) — the same queries that dec_region OOMs on at SF=100. On the apples-to-apples 15Q SF=20 intersection: enc=0.87x (slight regression!), dec=1.07x — same shape as SF=100. Sync still dominates kernel everywhere (sync/k = 4-5x at SF=100, 3-9x at SF=20).
